title: impatiens nzoana subsp. bennae
intro: Impatiens nzoana subsp. bennae, also known as the "Bennae touch-me-not," is a plant species within the balsam family (Balsamaceae) that is native to the tropical regions of Africa. The medicinal properties of Impatiens nzoana subsp. bennae include its ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Impatiens nzoana subsp. bennae include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
